title: MEDIUM: scope check uses substring matching (scope bypass)
description: In src/api.rs line 248, require_scope() uses self.scopes.contains(scope). This is a substring match, not a word-boundary match. A token with scope 'readonly' would pass require_scope('read'). A token with scope 'readwrite' would pass require_scope('read') and require_scope('write'). While current code only issues standard scopes ('read', 'write', 'read write'), any future custom scope containing a shorter scope name as a substring would be silently allowed. Fix: split scopes on whitespace and check membership, e.g. self.scopes.split_whitespace().any(|s| s == scope).
close_reason: Fixed: require_scope() now uses split_whitespace + exact match instead of substring contains
